result:
Filled all 50 decisions and wrote the YAML to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/agent-output/astrolabe-mvp-part-1.yaml`.

A few notes on the distribution:

- **High confidence (7):** D7 (forced — only one option), D8, D10, D14, D17, D18, D26, D35 — each pinned cleanly by a single principle (mostly #18 "earn from second consumer" and #2 "fail loud").
- **Low confidence (2):** D21 (brief-writ description — no strong principle), D31 (empty-decisions handling — principle #4 vs #2 tension; picked fall-through).
- **No custom answers.** The option frames were adequate throughout — no decision required re-framing.

Recurring principle citations: **#18** (earn structure from second consumer) drove many MVP-scoping calls; **#2** (fail loud) drove validation/permission/error-path calls; **#13** (first live writer sets precedent) drove convention-matching calls where Clerk/Spider/writ-* already established patterns.

One call worth flagging: **D1** went against the architecture spec. The spec said `@shardworks/astrolabe` (no suffix); principle #13 says existing `-apparatus` packages are the live writers of this naming slot. I applied principle #13 but confidence is only medium — if the spec-writer had a named reason for divergence, this would flip.
